zh.lifeSummary: Olive Rarick是Isaac Rarick的女兒，於1819年出生在美國紐約州。她於1837年10月17日與Franklin Vinton在美國俄亥俄州奧加湯普森結婚。他們至少是6個兒子和3個女兒的父母。她於1850年住在美國印第安納州瓦巴申諾布爾 堂史普。她去世於1860年12月18日享年41歲，埋葬在美國密西根州貝里恩維薩福 堂史普格倫多拉格倫多拉 其特利。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Olive Rarick是Isaac Rarick的女儿，于1819年出生在美国纽约州。她于1837年10月17日与Franklin Vinton在美国俄亥俄州奧加湯普森结婚。他们至少是6个儿子和3个女儿的父母。她于1850年住在美国印第安纳州瓦巴申諾布爾 堂史普。她去世于1860年12月18日享年41岁，埋葬在美国密歇根州貝里恩維薩福 堂史普格倫多拉格倫多拉 其特利。

variants.name: Roehrig
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Gerd, Hans, Frieda, Jutta, Manfred.
topographic name from Middle High German rōrach, rœrach ‘reed bed’, or a habitational name from any of the four places called with this word.

nameMeanings.name: Olive
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Originally a medieval given name, Oliff(e), derived via French from the Late Latin name Oliva, which was borne by two obscure early saints. It in turn is derived from the Latin name for the olive tree, and was no doubt adopted at first because of the associations of the olive tree with peaceful productivity and fruitfulness; the olive branch has been a symbol of peace since biblical times. Like many names denoting plants and flowers, it was particularly popular in the early decades of the 20th century. See also Olivia .

historicEvents.title: State Capital Moves to Indianapolis
historicEvents.description: The state capital was moved from Corydon to Indianapolis on January 10, 1825.

historicEvents.title: Slavery Becomes Illegal in New York State
historicEvents.description: During the years 1799 to 1827, New York went through a period of gradual emancipation. A Gradual Emancipation Law was passed in 1799 which freed slave children born after July 4, 1799. However, they were indentured until 25 years old for women and 28 years old for men. A law passed 1817 which freed slaves born before 1799, yet delayed their emancipation for ten years. All remaining slaves were freed in New York State on July 4, 1827.
Historical Boundaries 1832:  Wabash, Indiana, United States
U.S. acquires vast tracts of Mexican territory in wake of Mexican War including California and New Mexico.

historicEvents.title: Moving the State Capital
historicEvents.description: The capital of Michigan was moved from Detroit to Lansing on March 17, 1847. The capital was moved to be further away from Canada, to encourage settlement and boost economy toward the inner regions of the state, and to make to capital more accessible to everyone statewide.

historicEvents.title: Creation of the Republican Party
historicEvents.description: A debate continues over the location of the creation of the Republican Party. Some sources claim the party was formed in Ripon, Wisconsin, on February 28, 1854. Others claim the first meeting of the Republican Party took place in Jackson, Michigan, on July 6, 1854, where the Republican Party was officially organized. Over 1,000 people were present and candidates were selected for the party, thus making it the first Republican convention.
historicEvents.urlTitle: The Birthplace of the Republican Party

historicEvents.title: The Town of Santa Claus
historicEvents.description: The town of Santa Fe was denied their application for a United States Postal Service as a town of Santa Fe, Indiana, was already established. Several meetings were held and the name was changed to Santa Claus, Indiana. The United States Postal Service granted their application. Due to the name, the post office in Santa Claus continues to receive thousands of letters to Santa Claus from children around the world each December.

en.lifeSummary: Olive Rarick was born in 1819, in New York, United States as the daughter of Isaac Rarick. She married Franklin Vinton on 17 October 1837, in Thompson, Geauga, Ohio, United States. They were the parents of at least 6 sons and 3 daughters. She lived in Noble Township, Wabash, Indiana, United States in 1850. She died on 18 December 1860, at the age of 41, and was buried in Glendora Cemetery, Glendora, Weesaw Township, Berrien, Michigan, United States.

Ils ont eu au moins 6 fils et 3 filles. Elle a vécu à Noble Township, Wabash, Indiana, États-Unis en 1850. ja.lifeSummary: Olive Rarickは1819年にアメリカ合衆国ニューヨーク州でIsaac Rarickの娘として生まれた。1837年10月17日にアメリカ合衆国オハイオ州ジェアウガソンプソンでFranklin Vintonと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも6人の息子と3人の娘の親であった。彼女は1850年にはアメリカ合衆国インディアナ州ワバッシュノブル トウンシュイップに住んでいた。彼女は1860年12月18日に41歳で死去し，アメリカ合衆国ミシガン州バーリーンウィーサウ トウンシュイップグレンドラグレンドラ セメテリーにて埋葬された。

ko.Noble, Wabash, Indiana, United States: 미국 인디애나 주 와바시 노블일 타운시프
ko.Glendora Cemetery, Weesaw Township, Berr: 미국 미시간 주 베리 위사w 타운시프 글렌도라 글렌도라 세멧리
ko.lifeSummary: Olive Rarick(은)는 1819년 미국 뉴욕 주에서 Isaac Rarick의 딸로 태어났다. 그녀는 1837년 10월 17일 미국 오하이오 주 그이가 톰프슨에서 Franklin Vinton(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 여섯 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 1850년도에 미국 인디애나 주 와바시 노블일 타운시프에서 살았다. 그녀는 1860년 12월 18일 41세의 나이로 사망했으며 미국 미시간 주 베리 위사w 타운시프 글렌도라 글렌도라 세멧리에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Olive Rarick nasceu em 1819, em Nova Iorque, Estados Unidos da América filha de Isaac Rarick. Ela casou-se com Franklin Vinton em 17 de outubro de 1837, em Thompson, Geauga, Ohio, Estados Unidos da América. Eles tiveram pelo menos 6 filhos e 3 filhas. Ela viveu em Noble Township, Wabash, Indiana, Estados Unidos da América em 1850. Ela faleceu em 18 de dezembro de 1860, com 41 anos, e foi sepultada em Glendora Cemetery, Glendora, Weesaw Township, Berrien, Michigan, Estados Unidos da América.

ru.lifeSummary: Olive Rarick родилась в 1819 г. в местности Нью-Йорк, Соединенные штаты. Её отца звали Isaac Rarick. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Franklin Vinton 17 октября 1837 г. в местности томпсон, джога, Огайо, Соединенные штаты. У них было по крайней мере 6 сыновей и 3 дочери. Она жила в местности нобл тоуншип, вабаш, Индиана, Соединенные штаты в 1850 году. Она умерла 18 декабря 1860 г. в возрасте 41 год и была похоронена в местности глендора кеметири, глендора, висав тоуншип, бирриен, Мичиган, Соединенные штаты.

es.lifeSummary: Olive Rarick nació en 1819, en Nueva York, Estados Unidos como hija de Isaac Rarick. Se casó con Franklin Vinton el 17 de octubre de 1837, en Thompson, Geauga, Ohio, Estados Unidos. Fueron padres de por lo menos 6 hijos y 3 hijas. Vivió en Noble Township, Wabash, Indiana, Estados Unidos en 1850. Murió el 18 de diciembre de 1860, a la edad de 41 años, y fue sepultada en Glendora Cemetery, Glendora, Weesaw Township, Berrien, Michigan, Estados Unidos.
